enrichment.short_description: Actual Budget is a free, open-source, privacy-focused personal finance app built around envelope budgeting, with bank sync, multi-device support, and self-hosted or managed sync options.
enrichment.seo_title: Actual Budget: Free, Open-Source Envelope Budgeting
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Free, open-source envelope budgeting app with bank sync, multi-device access, and self-hosted or managed hosting for privacy-focused personal finance.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Actual Budget is a free, open-source personal finance application built around envelope-style budgeting, where users allocate actual income to spending categories. The project is developed by the Actual Budget open-source community and distributed via GitHub, with apps for web, desktop (Mac, Windows, Linux via Electron), iOS, and Android. It is local-first: data lives on the user's device and syncs across devices through a sync server that users either self-host or run via a third-party managed host such as PikaPods, with optional end-to-end encryption for synced data. Core features include bank account syncing (via GoCardless for the EU/UK and SimpleFIN for the US/Canada), transaction imports in QIF, OFX, QFX, CAMT.053, and CSV formats, custom reports, net worth tracking, undo/redo, dark mode, and a full API for developers who want to build on top of it. It also supports data migration from YNAB4 and nYNAB, making it a common destination for users leaving subscription-based budgeting apps. Because it is entirely free and open source, there are no subscription tiers or paywalled features; the only recurring cost, if any, comes from optionally paying a third party to host the sync server. Actual Budget suits privacy-conscious individuals and households who want full control over their financial data and are comfortable with a self-hosted or semi-technical setup, as an alternative to commercial budgeting apps like YNAB.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Actual Budget is an open-source personal finance app that implements envelope-style budgeting, where every dollar of actual income is assigned to a category before it's spent. It positions itself as a fast, local-first, privacy-focused alternative to subscription budgeting apps.

The app is developed openly on GitHub by a community of contributors and is free to use. Because it has no company-run backend by default, users choose how to run the sync layer: fully self-hosted, or through a managed hosting provider such as PikaPods.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Actual Budget supports multi-device sync with optional end-to-end encryption, bank account syncing through GoCardless (EU/UK) and SimpleFIN (US/Canada), and transaction imports from QIF, OFX, QFX, CAMT.053, and CSV files.
It also includes custom reporting, net worth tracking, undo/redo history, a dark mode, a documented API for developers, and built-in tools to migrate existing budgets from YNAB4 or nYNAB.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Actual Budget itself is free with no paid tiers. Users who don't want to run their own sync server can pay a third-party host, such as PikaPods, for managed hosting; a portion of those hosting fees is donated to the project's Open Collective.

features.title: Envelope budgeting
features.description: Allocate actual income to spending categories rather than budgeting projected income.

features.title: Bank syncing
features.description: Connect bank accounts via GoCardless (EU/UK) or SimpleFIN (US/Canada) for automatic transaction imports.

features.title: Multi-device sync with encryption
features.description: Sync budgets across devices with optional end-to-end encryption on the sync server.

features.title: Flexible file imports
features.description: Import transactions from QIF, OFX, QFX, CAMT.053, and CSV files.

features.title: Custom reports & net worth tracking
features.description: Build custom reports and track net worth over time.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: A documented API lets developers build integrations or automations on top of Actual Budget.

pros: Completely free and open source with no subscription cost
Privacy-focused: local-first data with optional end-to-end encrypted sync
Flexible hosting: self-host or use a managed provider
Direct migration path from YNAB4/nYNAB
cons: Sync requires either running your own server or paying a third party, unlike turnkey commercial apps
Smaller community and support ecosystem than established paid budgeting apps
Bank sync providers vary by region and may not cover every bank

faqs.question: Is Actual Budget free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Actual Budget is free and open source. The only potential cost is optional third-party hosting for the sync server.

faqs.question: Do I need to self-host Actual Budget?
faqs.answer: You need a sync server for multi-device use, which you can self-host or run through a managed host like PikaPods; the app itself works locally without one.

faqs.question: Can I migrate from YNAB?
faqs.answer: Yes, Actual Budget includes built-in tools to import budgets from YNAB4 and nYNAB.

faqs.question: Is my financial data private?
faqs.answer: Actual Budget is local-first and supports optional end-to-end encryption for data synced between devices.

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious personal budgeting
use_cases.description: Manage household finances with envelope budgeting while keeping full control of where data is stored.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted finance stack
use_cases.description: Run a budgeting app alongside other self-hosted services without recurring subscription fees.

use_cases.title: Switching from YNAB
use_cases.description: Migrate an existing YNAB4 or nYNAB budget to a free, open-source alternative.

features: Bring your own server for multi-device sync

enrichment.short_description: Acuity Scheduling is an online appointment booking platform, owned by Squarespace, that lets businesses offer 24/7 self-scheduling, payments, and automated client reminders.
enrichment.headquarters: Allentown, Pennsylvania, US (now part of Squarespace)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Online booking software from Squarespace offering client self-scheduling, payments, calendar sync, and automated reminders for service businesses.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Acuity Scheduling is an online appointment scheduling and booking platform originally founded in 2006 and acquired by Squarespace in April 2019. It gives service-based businesses a branded booking page where clients can self-schedule appointments around the clock, along with tools to manage calendars, collect payments, and reduce no-shows. Key features include customizable booking pages, custom intake forms, calendar sync with Google and Apple Calendar to prevent double-booking, payment processing through Stripe, Square, or PayPal, automated email and text reminders, deposit collection and cancellation policies, staff and multi-location scheduling, and revenue tools such as packages, memberships, gift certificates, and coupons. It integrates with services including Zoom, Google Meet, QuickBooks, and Zapier, and offers a mobile app for managing bookings on the go. Acuity is priced on three paid tiers — Starter, Standard, and Premium — that scale by number of calendars and unlock features like text reminders, membership sales, HIPAA compliance (with a signed BAA), branding removal, and API/CSS customization on the top tier; each plan starts with a short free trial and no credit card required. It serves a wide range of industries, from solo coaches, therapists, and stylists to larger multi-staff wellness, fitness, and consulting businesses that need reliable self-service booking and payment collection.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Acuity Scheduling is an online appointment booking platform that gives businesses a branded scheduling page clients can use to book appointments at any time. Founded in 2006, it was acquired by Squarespace in 2019 and now operates as part of the Squarespace family of products.
The platform is built for service-based businesses that need to manage bookings, collect payments, and reduce no-shows without manual back-and-forth scheduling.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Acuity provides customizable booking pages, custom client intake forms, calendar sync to prevent double-booking, and payment processing via Stripe, Square, or PayPal.

It also includes no-show prevention through deposits and cancellation policies, staff and multi-location scheduling, and revenue features like packages, memberships, subscriptions, gift certificates, and coupons.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Acuity offers three paid plans: Starter at $16/month, Standard at $27/month, and Premium at $49/month (annual billing; monthly billing costs more). Higher tiers add more calendars, text reminders, membership/package sales, HIPAA compliance, branding removal, and API access. Every plan includes a short free trial with no credit card required.

long_form_blocks.heading: Integrations
paragraphs: Acuity connects with Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, Stripe, Square, PayPal, QuickBooks, Zoom, Google Meet, Squarespace, and Zapier, among other tools.

features.title: Branded booking pages
features.description: Customizable, 24/7 client self-scheduling pages.

features.title: Calendar sync
features.description: Two-way sync with Google and Apple Calendar to prevent double-booking.

features.title: Payment processing
features.description: Accept payments via Stripe, Square, or PayPal at time of booking.

features.title: No-show prevention
features.description: Deposits, cancellation policies, and automated email/text reminders.

features.title: Staff & location management
features.description: Manage availability across multiple staff members and locations.

features.title: Revenue tools
features.description: Sell packages, memberships, subscriptions, and gift certificates.

pros: Deep customization of booking pages and client workflows
Wide range of native integrations, including payments and video conferencing
Backed by Squarespace's infrastructure and support
Built-in tools for selling packages, memberships, and gift certificates
cons: Pricing scales with number of calendars, which can get costly for larger teams
HIPAA compliance and full branding removal are limited to the top-tier Premium plan
No permanently free plan — only a short trial period

faqs.question: Who owns Acuity Scheduling?
faqs.answer: Acuity Scheduling is owned by Squarespace, which acquired the company in April 2019.

faqs.question: Does Acuity Scheduling have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No permanent free plan; it offers a short free trial and requires a paid subscription afterward.

faqs.question: Can Acuity process payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with Stripe, Square, and PayPal for accepting payments at booking.

faqs.question: Is Acuity Scheduling HIPAA compliant?
faqs.answer: HIPAA compliance, including BAA signing, is available on the Premium plan.

use_cases.title: Solo service providers
use_cases.description: Coaches, therapists, and stylists managing their own booking calendar and payments.

use_cases.title: Multi-staff wellness businesses
use_cases.description: Spas, salons, and fitness studios coordinating bookings across multiple staff and locations.

use_cases.title: Healthcare and HIPAA-sensitive scheduling

enrichment.short_description: Adalo is a no-code platform for designing and publishing native iOS, Android, and web apps using a drag-and-drop visual builder, a hosted database, and an AI assistant.
enrichment.seo_title: Adalo: No-Code App Builder for iOS, Android & Web
enrichment.seo_meta_description: No-code platform for building and publishing native iOS, Android, and web apps with a drag-and-drop builder, hosted database, and AI assistant.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Adalo is a no-code app development platform founded in 2018 and headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, that lets users design, build, and publish native iOS and Android apps as well as responsive web apps without writing code. Its core interface is a multi-screen, drag-and-drop visual canvas paired with pre-built components, feature templates (for things like scheduling, payments, and messaging), and a component marketplace for extending functionality. Adalo includes an AI assistant called Ada that can generate and edit parts of an app from prompts. Data can be stored in Adalo's built-in hosted Postgres database or connected to external sources such as Airtable and Google Sheets, and apps can integrate with thousands of external services through direct connections as well as Zapier and Make. From a single project, users can publish simultaneously to the Apple App Store, Google Play Store, and the web. Pricing follows a freemium model: a Free plan supports up to 500 records and unlimited test apps, while paid plans (Starter, Professional, and Team, billed monthly or annually) unlock published apps, custom domains, automated app store publishing, more editors, integrations, and features like push notifications, custom formulas, and white-labeling. Adalo is aimed at non-technical founders, small businesses, freelancers, agencies, and larger organizations that want to build and ship real mobile and web apps without hiring developers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Adalo is a no-code platform, founded in 2018, that lets users visually design and publish native iOS and Android apps as well as web apps. It combines a drag-and-drop canvas with an AI assistant called Ada that can generate or edit parts of an app from a prompt.
Apps built in Adalo can be published from a single project to the Apple App Store, Google Play Store, and a custom web domain.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform offers a multi-screen visual canvas, a hosted Postgres database (or connections to external data sources like Airtable and Google Sheets), a component marketplace, and pre-built templates for common app features such as scheduling, payments, and messaging.
Adalo also supports direct integrations with services like Zapier, Make, Stripe, Gmail, and HubSpot, so apps can connect to external tools without custom backend code.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Adalo uses a freemium model. The Free plan allows up to 500 records and unlimited test apps but doesn't support publishing. Paid plans start at Starter ($36/month, one published app), Professional ($52/month, two published apps and more advanced features), and Team ($160/month, five published apps with white-labeling and priority support), all billed annually. Adalo states there are no usage- or token-based charges on top of these plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Adalo targets non-technical founders, small business owners, freelancers, and agencies building client apps, as well as larger organizations that need to publish multiple apps with team collaboration and white-labeling.

features.title: Drag-and-drop app builder
features.description: Visual, multi-screen canvas for designing app layouts without code.

features.title: AI assistant (Ada)
features.description: Generate and edit app screens or logic from natural-language prompts.

features.title: Hosted database
features.description: Built-in Postgres database, or connect external sources like Airtable and Google Sheets.

features.title: Native app publishing
features.description: Publish to the Apple App Store and Google Play Store from one project.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Direct connections to services like Zapier, Make, Stripe, Gmail, and HubSpot.

features.title: Templates and component marketplace
features.description: Pre-built feature templates and an extensible library of free and premium components.

pros: True no-code path to publishing native iOS and Android apps
Free tier available for prototyping before committing to a paid plan
Built-in database plus support for external data sources
AI assistant speeds up building common app screens
cons: Publishing to app stores and the web requires a paid plan
Record limits on lower tiers can be restrictive for data-heavy apps
Costs rise with number of published apps and team editors

faqs.question: Is Adalo really no-code?
faqs.answer: Yes, apps are built visually with a drag-and-drop canvas and pre-built components, without writing code.

faqs.question: Can I publish to the App Store and Google Play?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid plans support automated publishing to both app stores from a single project.

faqs.question: Does Adalo have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports up to 500 records and unlimited test apps, but published apps require a paid plan.

faqs.question: What is Ada?
faqs.answer: Ada is Adalo's built-in AI assistant that can generate or edit app screens and logic from prompts.

use_cases.title: Client app development
use_cases.description: Agencies and freelancers building apps for clients using reusable templates and components.

use_cases.title: Internal or small business apps
use_cases.description: Small businesses building scheduling, booking, or data-tracking apps for their own operations.

enrichment.short_description: ADempiere is a free, open-source ERP and CRM business suite covering finance, supply chain, and manufacturing, self-hosted and maintained by a community since 2006.
enrichment.seo_title: ADempiere: Free Open-Source ERP & CRM Suite
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Free, open-source ERP and CRM business suite for finance, supply chain, and manufacturing, self-hosted and maintained by a global community.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ADempiere is a free, open-source ERP (enterprise resource planning) and CRM business suite created in 2006 as a community fork of the Compiere ERP project. It is not run by a single company but maintained by an independent, bazaar-style open-source community with its own governance structure, including a community council, code of conduct, and project charter, supported by an association with members and a board. The software covers core business processes including finance, materials/supply chain management, manufacturing, and system administration, and includes a newer JavaScript-based user interface built with Vue. It is self-hosted, with deployment options including Docker, Helm, and Kubernetes Operator, giving organizations full control over their data and infrastructure without vendor lock-in or per-user licensing fees. Documentation covers developer guides, system administration, and functional tutorials, and the project has an active Discord community along with a history of in-person European developer conferences. Being fully open source under a copyleft license, ADempiere has no paid tiers or subscription plans; all costs come from self-hosting infrastructure and any optional third-party implementation or support services. It is best suited for small and mid-sized businesses, developers, and system integrators who want a customizable, self-hosted ERP/CRM alternative to proprietary commercial ERP systems and are comfortable managing their own deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ADempiere is a free, open-source ERP and CRM business suite that began in 2006 as a community fork of the Compiere ERP project. It is developed and governed by an independent open-source community rather than a single company.

The suite covers core business processes such as finance, materials/supply chain management, and manufacturing, and is designed to help organizations avoid vendor lock-in and licensing fees associated with proprietary ERP systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ADempiere provides business process management, materials management, and system administration tools, along with a newer Vue-based JavaScript user interface. It supports modern deployment methods including Docker, Helm, and a Kubernetes Operator.

long_form_blocks.heading: Development & Community
paragraphs: The project operates under a 'bazaar-style' open governance model, with a community council, code of conduct, and project charter, backed by an association with members and a board. It maintains active documentation and a Discord community, and has held in-person European developer conferences.

features.title: Finance & accounting
features.description: Core financial management as part of the ERP suite.

features.title: Materials & supply chain management
features.description: Tools for managing inventory and supply chain processes.

features.title: Manufacturing management
features.description: Support for manufacturing and production processes.

features.title: Vue-based UI
features.description: A modern JavaScript user interface built with Vue.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Self-host via Docker, Helm, or a Kubernetes Operator.

features.title: Community governance
features.description: Open, community-driven development with a council, code of conduct, and project charter.

pros: Completely free and open source with no licensing fees
Avoids vendor lock-in common with proprietary ERP systems
Highly customizable and self-hostable
Active, long-running open-source community since 2006
cons: Requires self-hosting and technical expertise to deploy and maintain
Steeper learning curve than commercial SaaS ERP products
Documentation and support are community-driven rather than vendor-backed

faqs.question: Is ADempiere free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, ADempiere is fully open source and free, with no licensing fees.

faqs.question: Is ADempiere self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is deployed on your own infrastructure, with support for Docker, Helm, and Kubernetes Operator deployments.

faqs.question: Who maintains ADempiere?
faqs.answer: It is maintained by an independent open-source community with its own governance council, rather than a single company.

faqs.question: What is ADempiere's history?
faqs.answer: It began in 2006 as a community fork of the Compiere ERP project.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted ERP for SMBs
use_cases.description: Small and mid-sized businesses wanting a full ERP/CRM suite without per-user licensing costs.

use_cases.description: System integrators and developers building tailored business software on an open ERP foundation.

enrichment.short_description: AdEspresso is a Facebook and Instagram ad management platform, now owned by Hootsuite, that helps businesses create, test, and optimize paid social campaigns from one dashboard.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Facebook and Instagram ad management platform from Hootsuite offering campaign creation, split testing, and automated optimization tools.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: AdEspresso is a digital advertising platform founded in 2012 that focuses on creating, managing, and optimizing Facebook and Instagram ad campaigns. It was acquired by social media management company Hootsuite in February 2017 and continues to operate as part of Hootsuite's advertising tools. AdEspresso lets users build campaigns across multiple ad accounts from a centralized dashboard, run split tests to compare ad variations, and apply automated optimization rules and performance triggers to shift budget toward better-performing ads. It supports collaboration features such as client approval workflows and role-based account access, reporting exports (web, PDF, email, Excel), and, on higher tiers, white-label reporting and Salesforce contacts sync. Pricing is subscription-based across three tiers: Starter at $49/month (up to $1,000 in monthly ad spend), Plus at $99/month (unlimited ad spend, more team seats, and multi-page bulk creation), and Enterprise starting at $259/month (unlimited ad spend, dedicated marketing consultant, monthly strategy sessions, and API access). All plans include a 14-day free trial. AdEspresso is aimed at digital marketing agencies managing multiple client ad accounts, e-commerce businesses, and SMBs that want to run and continuously optimize Facebook and Instagram advertising without manually managing campaigns inside Meta's native ads tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: AdEspresso is a Facebook and Instagram ad management platform founded in 2012. Hootsuite acquired the company in February 2017, and AdEspresso now operates as part of Hootsuite's advertising product line.
The platform is built to simplify creating, managing, and analyzing paid social campaigns across multiple ad accounts from a single dashboard.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: AdEspresso supports building campaigns across multiple channels and accounts simultaneously, split testing to compare ad variations, and automated optimization rules that shift budget based on performance triggers.
It also offers collaboration tools like client approval workflows, role-based access, and reporting exports in web, PDF, email, and Excel formats.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: AdEspresso is priced on three subscription tiers: Starter at $49/month (up to $1,000/month ad spend), Plus at $99/month (unlimited ad spend, more seats, campaign approval), and Enterprise starting at $259/month (unlimited ad spend, dedicated consultant, API access, Salesforce sync). All plans include a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Multi-account campaign creation
features.description: Build Facebook and Instagram campaigns across multiple ad accounts from one dashboard.

features.title: Split testing
features.description: Compare ad variations to identify top performers.

features.title: Automated optimization
features.description: Apply performance triggers that automatically shift budget toward better-performing ads.

features.title: Reporting & exports
features.description: Generate reports and export them as web, PDF, email, or Excel formats.

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: Client approval workflows and role-based account access.

features.title: White-label reporting
features.description: Available on higher tiers for agencies presenting results to clients.

pros: Strong split-testing and automated optimization tools
Centralized management of multiple Facebook/Instagram ad accounts
White-label reporting and collaboration features for agencies
Backed by Hootsuite's infrastructure
cons: No permanent free plan, only a 14-day trial
Focused solely on Facebook and Instagram, not other ad networks
Enterprise-level features and unlimited spend require the highest, priciest tier

faqs.question: Who owns AdEspresso?
faqs.answer: AdEspresso has been owned by Hootsuite since Hootsuite acquired it in February 2017.

faqs.question: Does AdEspresso have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No permanent free plan; it offers a 14-day free trial before requiring a paid subscription.

faqs.question: Which ad platforms does AdEspresso support?
faqs.answer: It focuses on Facebook and Instagram advertising campaigns.

faqs.question: What does the Starter plan include?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $49/month for up to $1,000 in monthly ad spend, with core campaign creation, split testing, and reporting features.

use_cases.title: Agency ad management
use_cases.description: Agencies managing Facebook and Instagram ads across multiple client accounts with approval workflows.

use_cases.title: E-commerce advertising
use_cases.description: Online stores running and optimizing paid social campaigns to drive sales.

use_cases.title: Split-test-driven campaign optimization
use_cases.description: Marketing teams testing ad variations and automating budget allocation based on performance.

enrichment.short_description: AdGuard Home is a free, open-source, self-hosted DNS server that blocks ads and trackers network-wide for every connected device, made by AdGuard Software.
enrichment.seo_title: AdGuard Home: Free Network-Wide Ad & Tracker Blocker
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted DNS server that blocks ads and trackers across your whole network, from AdGuard Software.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: AdGuard Home is a free, open-source, network-wide DNS-based ad and tracker blocker made by AdGuard Software Ltd, a company founded in 2009 and headquartered in Limassol, Cyprus. Instead of installing a browser extension per device, AdGuard Home is installed once on a router, computer, or device like a Raspberry Pi and filters DNS requests for every device on the network, blocking ads and redirecting tracking-domain lookups to prevent data exchange with tracking servers. It also offers parental controls, including content filtering and Safe Search enforcement, support for custom filtering rules for advanced users, and activity monitoring with network statistics. Because it is self-hosted, users run and control their own instance rather than relying on a third-party cloud service; the source code is publicly available on GitHub. AdGuard Home supports Windows, macOS, and Linux natively, runs well on low-power hardware like the Raspberry Pi, and offers an official Docker image along with packages for Snap, Home Assistant, and OpenWrt. It is free with no paid tier, distinct from AdGuard's separate cloud-hosted DNS product, AdGuard DNS. AdGuard Home is best suited for privacy-conscious users, home labbers, and small organizations who want to block ads and trackers for an entire network from a single, self-managed point rather than device by device.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: AdGuard Home is a free, open-source, network-wide ad and tracker blocker built by AdGuard Software Ltd, founded in 2009 and headquartered in Limassol, Cyprus. Rather than blocking ads per device or browser, it operates as a DNS server that filters requests for every device on a network.

It is self-hosted, meaning users install and run it themselves on hardware such as a router, home server, or Raspberry Pi, giving full control over filtering without depending on a third-party cloud service.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: AdGuard Home blocks ads and redirects tracking-domain lookups at the DNS level for browsers and apps alike. It includes parental controls such as content filtering and Safe Search enforcement, support for custom filtering rules, and activity monitoring with network statistics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Platforms & Deployment
paragraphs: AdGuard Home runs natively on Windows, macOS, and Linux, including low-power devices like the Raspberry Pi. It also offers an official Docker image and packages for platforms like Snap, Home Assistant, and OpenWrt, making it deployable in a wide range of home and small-network setups.

features.title: Network-wide DNS filtering
features.description: Blocks ads and trackers for every device on the network from a single install.

features.title: Custom filtering rules
features.description: Advanced users can add personalized blocklist rules.

features.title: Parental controls
features.description: Content filtering and Safe Search enforcement.

features.title: Activity monitoring
features.description: Network activity tracking and statistics dashboard.

features.title: Cross-platform support
features.description: Runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, and low-power devices like Raspberry Pi.

features.title: Docker support
features.description: Official Docker image for containerized deployment.

pros: Completely free and open source
Blocks ads and trackers across an entire network without per-device apps
Full control via self-hosting, including on low-power hardware
Runs well on inexpensive hardware like a Raspberry Pi
cons: Requires some technical setup to install and maintain your own server
No fully managed cloud option under this product (that's AdGuard's separate AdGuard DNS product)
Effectiveness depends on network configuration, such as devices using the configured DNS server

faqs.question: Is AdGuard Home free?
faqs.answer: Yes, AdGuard Home is free and open source with no paid tier.

faqs.question: Do I need to self-host AdGuard Home?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's designed to be installed and run on your own hardware, such as a router, home server, or Raspberry Pi.

faqs.question: What platforms does AdGuard Home support?
faqs.answer: It runs natively on Windows, macOS, and Linux, and supports Docker and Raspberry Pi deployments.

faqs.question: How is AdGuard Home different from a browser ad blocker?
faqs.answer: It filters DNS requests at the network level, blocking ads and trackers for every connected device rather than a single browser.

use_cases.title: Home network ad and tracker blocking
use_cases.description: Block ads and trackers across every device on a home network from one place.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted network filtering
use_cases.description: Run AdGuard Home on a Raspberry Pi or home server as part of a self-hosted infrastructure setup.

use_cases.title: Small office network protection
use_cases.description: Provide network-wide ad blocking and content filtering for a small office without per-device software.

enrichment.short_description: Admidio is a free, open-source, self-hosted PHP application for managing memberships, events, documents, and communication for clubs, associations, and community organizations.
enrichment.seo_title: Admidio: Free Membership Management for Organizations
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted membership management software for clubs, associations, and community organizations.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Admidio is a free, open-source membership and organization management application, in active development since 2004, aimed at clubs, associations, and community groups. It is a self-hosted, PHP-based web application that organizations install on their own web space to manage members and administrative tasks online. Core functionality includes member databases with role-based access control, customizable membership lists exportable to Excel, PDF, and CSV, event management with online registration, photo galleries and albums, document and file sharing, CSV-based user import and updates, and an extensible plugin system. Access to member data can be restricted by role, letting organizations grant different permissions to group leaders, webmasters, and general members. Development is community-driven and ongoing, with an active presence on GitHub, forums, and social platforms such as Mastodon and Instagram. Because Admidio is fully open source, it has no licensing cost or paid tiers; the only costs involved are the web hosting and PHP environment needed to run it. It suits nonprofit clubs, sports associations, hobby groups, and other community organizations that want a purpose-built, self-hosted alternative to generic spreadsheet-based or commercial membership-management tools, particularly organizations comfortable managing their own PHP hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Admidio is a free, open-source membership and organization management application that has been in active development since 2004. It is designed specifically for associations, clubs, and community groups that need to manage members and administrative tasks online.
The software is self-hosted and built in PHP, so organizations install it on their own web hosting rather than using a hosted SaaS product.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Admidio includes a role-based member database with access controls, customizable and exportable membership lists (Excel, PDF, CSV), event management with online registration, photo galleries, document and file sharing, and CSV import/update tools for member data.
The platform supports plugins for extended functionality and lets organizations assign different rights to roles such as group leaders and webmasters.

long_form_blocks.heading: Development & Community
paragraphs: Admidio has been developed continuously since 2004, with an active open-source community maintaining the project on GitHub and supporting users through forums and social channels.

pros: Free and fully open source with no licensing cost
Purpose-built for clubs and associations, not a generic tool
Full data control through self-hosting
Long-running, actively maintained community project since 2004
cons: Requires your own PHP web hosting to run
Interface is less modern than some commercial membership platforms
Smaller ecosystem and support base than mainstream SaaS alternatives

faqs.question: Is Admidio free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Admidio is free and open source with no licensing fees.

faqs.question: Do I need to host Admidio myself?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's a self-hosted PHP application that you install on your own web hosting.

faqs.question: Who is Admidio for?
faqs.answer: It's designed for clubs, associations, and community organizations managing members, events, and documents.

faqs.question: Can Admidio manage event registrations?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes event management with online registration functionality.

enrichment.short_description: Adminer is a free, open-source database management tool distributed as a single PHP file, supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MS SQL, Oracle, and more.
enrichment.seo_title: Adminer: Free Single-File Database Management Tool
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Free, open-source, single-file PHP database management tool supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MS SQL, Oracle, and more.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Adminer is a free, open-source database management tool created by developer Jakub Vrána, first released in 2007 as a lighter-weight alternative to phpMyAdmin. It is distributed as a single PHP file, which can simply be uploaded to a PHP-enabled server to provide a full web-based database administration interface, without a separate installation process. Adminer supports MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MS SQL, Oracle, and CockroachDB natively, with additional support for databases like MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, and Firebird available through plugins. Core functionality includes browsing and editing table structures and data, running SQL commands, exporting databases, and managing indexes, foreign keys, views, stored procedures, triggers, and user permissions. It requires PHP 5.3 or later with sessions enabled, and community-maintained packages exist for deploying it via Docker, Debian, WordPress, and Drupal, among other environments. Adminer is released under a dual Apache 2.0 / GPL 2 license, making it free for both commercial and non-commercial use, with no paid tiers. It is best suited for developers and system administrators who want a lightweight, portable database management tool that can run on nearly any PHP host, particularly as an alternative to heavier tools like phpMyAdmin when working across multiple database engines.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Adminer is a free, open-source database management tool created by developer Jakub Vrána and first released in 2007 as a lightweight alternative to phpMyAdmin. Its defining feature is that the entire application ships as a single PHP file.

Because it requires no separate install process beyond uploading that file to a PHP-enabled server, Adminer is especially portable and easy to deploy on shared hosting or alongside other web applications.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Adminer supports browsing and editing table structures and data, running raw SQL commands, exporting databases, and managing indexes, foreign keys, views, stored procedures, triggers, and user/rights management.
It natively supports MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MS SQL, Oracle, and CockroachDB, with plugin-based support for additional systems like MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, and Firebird.

long_form_blocks.heading: Deployment
paragraphs: Adminer runs on any server with PHP 5.3+ and sessions enabled. Community-maintained packages make it easy to deploy via Docker, Debian, WordPress, Drupal, and other platforms.

features.title: Single-file deployment
features.description: The entire tool is a single PHP file that can be uploaded directly to a server.

features.title: Multi-database support
features.description: Native support for MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MS SQL, Oracle, and CockroachDB.

features.title: Table & data editing
features.description: Browse, edit, search, sort, and filter table data and structure.

features.title: SQL command execution
features.description: Run raw SQL commands directly against the database.

features.title: Export functionality
features.description: Export databases and query results.

features.title: Plugin extensibility
features.description: Add support for databases like MongoDB, Redis, and Elasticsearch via plugins.

pros: Extremely lightweight and portable as a single PHP file
Free and open source under Apache 2.0 / GPL 2 dual license
Broad database support, including through plugins
Actively maintained with regular releases
cons: Requires a PHP server environment to run
Less feature-rich UI than dedicated desktop database clients
Care is needed when exposing it on a public-facing server, as with any web-based DB admin tool

faqs.question: Is Adminer free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Adminer is free and open source, licensed under Apache 2.0 / GPL 2, for both commercial and non-commercial use.

faqs.question: What databases does Adminer support?
faqs.answer: It natively supports MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MS SQL, Oracle, and CockroachDB, with plugins for others like MongoDB and Redis.

faqs.question: How is Adminer installed?
faqs.answer: It's a single PHP file that you upload to any PHP-enabled server; no separate installation process is required.

faqs.question: How does Adminer compare to phpMyAdmin?
faqs.answer: Adminer was created as a lighter-weight, single-file alternative to phpMyAdmin, supporting more database engines out of the box.

use_cases.title: Quick lightweight database administration
use_cases.description: Developers needing a fast, portable way to browse and edit databases without a full desktop client.

use_cases.title: Shared hosting database management
use_cases.description: Sysadmins managing MySQL or PostgreSQL databases on shared or budget hosting environments.

use_cases.title: Alternative to phpMyAdmin
use_cases.description: Teams wanting a lighter-weight, single-file alternative to phpMyAdmin across multiple database engines.
